objective: Approve and document the governance standard for borrowing installed dependencies from a root checkout into isolated worktrees via symlinks, and issue-back any helper follow-up before closure.
scope_boundary: Review existing governance standards for any documented worktree dependency-sharing guidance.
Document the approved allowlist, forbidden artifacts, verification checks, and cleanup expectations if the pattern is allowed.
Create a follow-up issue if helper automation is still needed after the manual pattern is documented.
out_of_scope: Implementing the helper itself in this slice.
research_summary: Governance already documented isolated worktrees and one adjacent graphify hook/worktree failure pattern, but it did not explicitly govern borrowing installed dependencies from a root checkout into a clean issue worktree. The pattern is already useful in practice, so the correct move is to approve it narrowly with same-repo and lockfile verification, then open a separate helper issue because the manual setup remains error-prone.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Land the governance standard for the manual symlink pattern.
tasks: Review current governance text for any existing guidance on shared dependency symlinks in clean worktrees.
Document allowed artifacts, forbidden artifacts, pre-link verification, post-link verification, and cleanup expectations in STANDARDS.md.
Record the approved policy in OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md.
acceptance_criteria: Governance has an explicit documented position on the pattern.
The standard names allowed artifacts, forbidden artifacts, verification requirements, and cleanup expectations.
The backlog records the approved policy as completed governance work.

sprints.name: Sprint 2
sprints.goal: Capture any remaining work before closing the issue.
tasks: Decide whether a helper/runbook follow-up is needed after documenting the manual pattern.
Create a follow-up issue if helper work is still required.
Write the canonical structured JSON plan and PDCA/R closeout for this slice.
acceptance_criteria: Any helper or runbook follow-up is issue-backed before issue closure.
The slice has both a canonical JSON plan and a PDCA/R closeout artifact.

specialist_reviews.focus: Validate that the symlink pattern should be approved narrowly for dependency installs only, rather than left undocumented or generalized too broadly.
specialist_reviews.summary: The pattern is already useful in isolated worktrees, but only if it is constrained to same-repo dependency artifacts with lockfile parity checks and explicit cleanup. Secrets, repo metadata, and build outputs must remain forbidden.

alternate_model_review.summary: This is a bounded governance-standard slice and did not require an alternate-model review to proceed.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Proceed with the governance standard update for shared dependency symlinks in isolated worktrees and create any required helper follow-up before closure.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Proof the documented pattern on a real worktree, write the PDCA/R closeout, and merge the governance documentation update.

material_deviation_rules: If worktree proof shows hidden risks, tighten the standard in the same slice or create a blocker follow-up issue before closure.
If repeated manual setup is clearly too error-prone, create the helper issue before moving the governance issue to done.
